# Figure First

## Intent
Let a single dominant figure do the teaching before prose names what it shows.

## Best For
- Biology, chemistry, geography, and anatomy lessons
- Topics where the learner needs a spatial anchor before vocabulary lands
- Visual or mixed-modality classrooms

## Not Ideal For
- Topics with little visual structure
- Proof-style or text-dense formal lessons

## Learner Fit
- Visual
- General

## Subjects
- Biology
- Chemistry
- Geography
- Science

## Lesson Flow
Hook -> Diagram -> Explain -> Process -> Practice -> What Next

## Required Components
- SectionHeader
- HookHero
- DiagramBlock
- ExplanationBlock
- PracticeStack
- WhatNextBridge

## Optional Components
- GlossaryRail
- ProcessSteps
- PitfallAlert

## Why This Template Exists
Some ideas become understandable the moment the learner can point at the structure. This template lets the figure do that work first.

## Responsive Notes
Keep the figure near the top on every breakpoint and stack the support blocks beneath it on smaller screens.

## Print Notes
Render the figure as a full-width static visual and expand any step-based support for print.
